entityContent: After a long discussion, we decided to migrate the authentication layer to OAuth2. The team agreed this would improve security and reduce maintenance overhead.
The board approved the Q3 budget proposal for infrastructure upgrades. All stakeholders were present and the vote was unanimous in favor of the plan.
We agreed to use PostgreSQL instead of MySQL for the new data warehouse. The decision was based on better JSON support and more flexible indexing options.
The team finalized the deployment schedule for the next release. We decided to use blue-green deployments to minimize downtime during the production cutover.
After evaluating three vendors, management approved the contract with Acme Corp for cloud hosting services. This covers a two-year commitment at the negotiated rate.
Going forward, all API endpoints must include rate limiting headers. This will help us better manage traffic and prevent abuse from automated clients.
Our approach will be to use feature flags for all new functionality. This gives us the ability to roll back quickly without a full deployment if issues arise.
Consensus: the team will adopt trunk-based development instead of the current gitflow process. Short-lived feature branches are preferred to reduce merge conflicts.
Let's go with the microservices architecture for the payment service. We'll start with three bounded contexts: billing, subscriptions, and invoices.
We're going with React for the frontend instead of Vue. The team has more experience with React and the ecosystem is more mature for our use case.
Action item: @alice will set up the CI/CD pipeline for the new staging environment before the end of the sprint. She will document the configuration steps as well.
TODO: @bob needs to update the database migration scripts to handle null values in legacy records. This must be completed by Friday before the release goes out.

[ ] Write unit tests for the new authentication module
All tests should cover both success and failure paths including edge cases for token expiry.
The performance optimization task is assigned to @carol and is due by end of week. She will profile the slow queries and add appropriate indexes to the main tables.
Due date: December 15. The security audit report needs to be completed and submitted to compliance. @dave will handle the final review and sign-off.
We decided to adopt GraphQL for the new API layer going forward. This will simplify client data fetching. Action item: @eve will create the initial schema definitions by end of month.
notes: mixed: explicit decision + going forward + action item

entityContent: Consensus: we're going with serverless functions for background jobs. @frank will migrate the first three jobs by next Friday as a proof of concept.
notes: mixed: consensus + going with + action with deadline
